system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Persistent Errors

We have two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cascading error — cannot find mesh because blockMesh failed

The blockMesh error has persisted across 3 attempts with different vertex orderings. The root cause is that **Block 0** `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` has all 8 vertices defining a flat rectangular prism (coplanar opposing faces), making it a degenerate hex block.

## New Approach — Completely Restructure the Geometry

Instead of trying to fix the existing vertex layout, **redesign the blockMeshDict to match the canonical squareBend tutorial topology**. The key insight is that the bend requires intermediate vertices placed at the inner and outer radii of the curve so that each hex block occupies a true 3D corner region (not a flat slab).

### Step-byistant
<think></think># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Problem Diagnosis

The blockMesh keeps failing because Block 0 `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` produces a degenerate hex — all 8 vertices lie on the surface of a rectangular prism, making the block geometrically invalid ("inside-out").

Previous attempts have tried different vertex orderings but the fundamental geometric issue remains: the vertex positions create flat, degenerate blocks.

## New Solution — Adopt the Standard SquareBend Tutorial Topology Exactly

The vertices and blocks need to be restructured so that **no single hex block spans the full width of the duct**. Instead, use multiple smaller blocks that fill the inlet duct and transition into the bend through proper 3D corners.

Here is the proposed fix for `system/blockMeshDict`:

### 1. Redefine vertices to include intermediate corner vertices

Add vertices at the inner and outer radii of the bend. Replace the current 20 vertices with approximately 24-28 vertices placing intermediate points at the bend corners. For example, add vertices at positions like (~`-25`, ~`-25`), (~`-25`, ~`0`), (~`0`, ~`-25`) to create proper L-shaped block transitions.

### 2. Redefine blocks as small non-degenerate hexahedra

Break the geometry into many small blocks (e.g., 6-8 blocks total) where each block occupies a localized corner of the bend or a straight section. Crucially, **each block must span a true 3D volume** — no block should have both opposing faces identical or coplanar.

A recommended block structure (following the squareBend tutorial):
- Several small blocks along the inlet duct (stacked in y-direction)
- Corner blocks using inner-radius vertices to turn the flow
- Outer-radius blocks to complete the bend transition
- Straight duct blocks leading to the outlet

Each block definition should look like:
```
hex (v_i v_j v_k v_l v_m v_n v_o v_p) (nx ny nz) simpleGrading (gx gy gz)
```
where the first 4 vertices form one face and the last 4 form the opposite face, with the vertex ordering following the right-hand rule.

### 3. Simplify the approach — Copy the exact vertex coordinates and block topology from the published squareBend tutorial

Since the user states "Follow the same geometry as the tutorial," the best approach is to **use the exact vertex coordinates and block definitions from the open-source squareBend tutorial** (which is well-tested and known to work). The current custom geometry with only 4 vertices per straight section and only 3 blocks for the bend is inherently problematic — it cannot produce valid hexahedral meshes.

The key difference: the tutorial uses **many more vertices and blocks** (typically 20+ vertices, 10-15 blocks) so that each hex block is a small, non-degenerate corner piece. The current design collapses multiple physical regions into single blocks that are geometrically degenerate.

## Summary

| File | Issue | Action |
|------|-------|--------|
| `system/blockMeshDict` | All block definitions produce degenerate/inverted hexahedra due to poor vertex layout and insufficient block count | Completely restructure vertices to include intermediate corner points and define 8-12 small non-overlapping blocks that fill the domain using proper right-hand-rule ordering. Base the topology on the canonical squareBend tutorial. |

The blockMesh error will resolve once valid non-degenerate hex blocks are defined. After blockMesh succeeds, the rhoSimpleFoam error will resolve automatically since the mesh will exist.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire file content with a corrected blockMeshDict that defines proper non-degenerate hex blocks following the standard squareBend tutorial topology; Add intermediate vertices at bend corners to enable 3D block shapes; Define blocks such that each spans a true 3D corner region not a flat slab; Use vertex ordering that follows the right-hand rule; Keep scale 1; Set up patches: inlet, outlet, upperWall, lowerWall, leftWall, rightWall; Ensure all blocks use simpleGrading (1 1 1) or appropriate grading; Use enough sub-divisions per block to resolve the bend smoothly'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
